title: Detour rules
description: Import/export format for the Detour extension (https://github.com/amitse/detour).

$schema.description: Optional pointer back to this schema. Editors that understand $schema (VS Code, etc.) will validate against it.

rule.description: A single redirect or script-injection rule.

id.description: Stable identifier. Generated if missing on import.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the popup.

type.description: redirect: rewrite the URL via declarativeNetRequest + in-page fetch/XHR patching. script: inject external JS into matching pages, bypassing CSP.

destination.description: Destination URL for redirect rules. $1, $2… reference captured groups from the source pattern. Empty for script rules.

scripts.description: Scripts to inject when type is "script". Empty for redirect rules.

operator.description: Use `wildcard` for new rules — `*` stands in for any text (leave it out for exact match, wrap a term in `*…*` for contains). `contains` and `equals` are legacy aliases and get normalized to wildcard on load. `regex` is accepted for power users but the extension UI does not create new regex rules.

value.description: Pattern to match against the request URL. For wildcard, each `*` is a capture group available as `$1`, `$2`… in the redirect destination.

method.description: Restrict matching to requests with this HTTP method. Defaults to all methods.

scriptEntry.description: One external script to fetch and inject.

src.description: HTTP/HTTPS URL of the script to fetch and inject.

attrs.description: Attributes applied to the injected <script> element (e.g. type=module, async, crossorigin).
